title: nasturtium hirtellum
intro: Nasturtium hirtellum, also known as the hairy watercress, is an annual herbaceous plant that belongs to the Brassicaceae family, characterized by its bright green leaves and small white flowers. The plant is rich in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as minerals such as potassium and iron, making it a valuable medicinal plant. Traditionally, nasturtium hirtellum has been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, wounds, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Nasturtium hirtellum can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and is also used as a fresh ingredient in salads and as a garnish.
